rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: v0.13.1 Lane E3 re-mint (was PHANTOM-GREEN, filed HIGH in SURPRISES-INTAKE.md v0.13.0): the verifier script never exported REPOSIX_SIM_ORIGIN, so `reposix init` inside the gate resolved against the hardcoded default 127.0.0.1:7878 instead of the gate's own sim bind 127.0.0.1:7779 -- the committed row's 2026-05-01 PASS predates the port change and was never re-verified since. Proven concretely this session: (1) with REPOSIX_SIM_ORIGIN unset, re-running the gate's `reposix init sim::demo` produced `error: cannot list issues for import: blocked origin: http://127.0.0.1:7878/projects/demo/issues` (egress-allowlist-blocked, since only 7779 is allowlisted) and the gate exited 1 -- i.e. the OLD script (absent this fix) concretely FAILs against the current codebase, not phantom-passes; (2) with the one-line `export REPOSIX_SIM_ORIGIN="${SIM_URL}"` fix added, `reposix init` logged `remote.origin.url = reposix::http://127.0.0.1:7779/projects/demo` (the gate's OWN sim, not the default) and the gate exited 0, teaching-string greps passed, artifact written to quality/reports/verifications/agent-ux/dark-factory-sim.json. transport_claim is FALSE per the same rationale as agent-ux/zero-shot-onboarding: this exercises the local sim-backed init/partial-clone flow, not a real external backend, so coverage_kind: mechanical documents that substrate honestly.

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The test spins a REAL reposix-sim axum process in-process (reposix_sim::run_with_listener) backed by a persistent SQLite file, pre-seeds it via the sim's own production reposix_sim::seed::apply_seed, drives a real push through git-remote-reposix, then opens that SAME sqlite file with rusqlite::Connection::open and queries audit_events directly (COUNT(*) WHERE method=... AND path=...) -- not a wiremock request-log proxy. If the backend-mutation audit row were silently dropped, patch_count would read 0 and the assert_eq!(patch_count, 1, ...) would fail concretely, naming the missing count. Run + confirmed PASS on this host (git 2.25.1) 2026-07-05 -- this scenario never touches git>=2.34 codepaths (plain git init/commit/push against a file:// mirror + the export capability), so it is NOT gated by the git-version floor.

Bug (SURPRISES-INTAKE.md ~L891-939, HIGH): meta::prune_oid_map (272882c/e246e84) DELETEs oid_map rows whose issue_id is absent from a keep_ids set built from self.backend.list_records(&self.project); GitHub/JIRA/Confluence connectors can silently return a TRUNCATED Ok(partial_list) at a pagination/size cap (github/lib.rs MAX_ISSUES_PER_LIST=500 / MAX_RAW_ITEMS_PER_LIST; jira/lib.rs non-strict list_issues_impl; Confluence equivalent), so a truncated keep_ids wipes oid_map rows for LIVE records beyond the cap — a real record looks ghost/deleted, recurring on EVERY sync; the sim never truncates so every sim-run gate (incl. all of P93's GREEN runs) is structurally blind. Fork B (defense-in-depth): reclassify delete-time NotFound as idempotent success in write_loop.rs, bounding residual ghost-row phantom DELETEs to audit noise. This is a sim/unit gate keyed off a capped-MOCK connector (transport_claim:false); it does NOT need a real backend.

rows.comment: P94 D2 — git-2.43 fallback-sentinel (HIGH carry-forward, SURPRISES-INTAKE.md L602-610). Stock Ubuntu 24.04 git (2.43.0) fails EVERY real single-backend `git push` outright (exit 128). CORRECTED MECHANISM (DP-2 container tracing with GIT_TRANSPORT_HELPER_DEBUG=1, see 94-D2-REPRO-NOTES.md — the original `stateless-connect git-receive-pack` / `fallback`-sentinel diagnosis this row's slug still carries was DISPROVEN): git 2.43 (and 2.54) NEVER probe `stateless-connect` for the push direction. The real, version-windowed blocker is `option object-format` — reposix's helper advertises the `object-format` capability, so git 2.43 sends `option object-format` and treats the helper's `unsupported` answer as FATAL (exit 128). The fix that actually unblocks the push is answering `option object-format` with `ok` in crates/reposix-remote/src/main.rs (commit c136795), NOT the fallback sentinel. The git-remote-helpers(7) `fallback` sentinel on the non-`git-upload-pack` `stateless-connect` branch is SPEC-COMPLIANCE only and is DEAD CODE for the push path: reposix emits it for conformance (and the asserts below still verify that emission), but git never reaches it during a push, so it was never the push-unblocking mechanism. Version-windowed: git 2.54.0 (this project's CI runner) does NOT hit it; the >= 2.34 floor does NOT protect (2.43 > 2.34 yet breaks). transport_claim is set FALSE (explicit opt-out): the transport under test is the LOCAL git<->helper protocol handshake (backend-agnostic, exercised against sim in a git-2.43 container), NOT a remote-backend transport claim — coverage_kind: mechanical documents the container/sim substrate; a real-backend push on git 2.43 is out of CI's reach (CI runs 2.54) and is the container repro's job here.

rows.comment: P104 / S-260707-gh404 -- cheap sim/unit regression guard for the GitHub helper-path 404 (RAISE #3, transport_claim:false, runs in `-p reposix-cache` with NO GitHub token). ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): `Cache` carried a SINGLE `project` field used for BOTH the on-disk cache dir AND the backend REST calls; every caller (helper main.rs:141, attach.rs:162, sync.rs:103) fed the filesystem-SANITIZED form (`owner/repo` -> `owner-repo`) to `Cache::open`, so `build_from` called `list_records_complete("owner-repo")` and the GitHub connector formatted `GET /repos/owner-repo/issues` -> 404. Only GitHub has a slash in its project slug, so only GitHub 404'd (sim/JIRA/Confluence unaffected). FIX (owner taste): the backend keeps the RAW slug; sanitization moves to the single on-disk path-derivation site (`reposix_core::path::sanitize_project_for_cache`, called inside `reposix_cache::path::resolve_cache_path`). `Cache::open` now stores the RAW slug in `self.project` (so REST calls get `owner/repo`) while the cache DIR is still `github-owner-repo.git`. This row's regression test pins BOTH halves of that split so a future re-sanitize of `self.project` (the exact regression) fails without needing a live GitHub call.

the on-disk cache path (cache.repo_path()) is STILL the sanitized flat dir "github-owner-repo.git" -- no embedded slash, no nested owner/ subdirectory (pins that path derivation sanitizes)
the test fails on the PRE-FIX resolve_cache_path (which did not sanitize -> nested github-owner/repo.git dir) and passes on the fixed one -- fails-then-passes demonstrated

rows.comment: RBF-LR-03 (P105) rebase-recovery reconciliation. Proves the documented `git pull --rebase && git push` recovery actually reconciles after SoT drift. Root cause: fast_import.rs emit_import_stream minted the client tracking commit refs/reposix/origin/main as a PARENTLESS commit, so any SoT tree change produced a non-descendant root that `git fast-import` refused (`does not contain`), aborting pull --rebase. Fix chains the synth commit onto the current tracking tip. Sim-backed (NOT a real backend), transport_claim:false - this is a client-side fast-import ref-update mechanic, not a transport-latency claim.

Scenario B (external REST PATCH drift): clone C `git pull --rebase` exits 0, `git push` converges
NEGATIVE GUARD: the pre-fix emit_import_stream (parentless) reproduces `fatal: error while running fast-import` - captured in the transcript as the RED baseline
emit_import_stream emits `from <parent>` when the tracking ref exists; parentless only on first fetch

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier drives the real git-remote-reposix helper against a live sim through two SoT-drift scenarios (peer git push, external REST PATCH) and asserts each clone's `git pull --rebase` exits 0 with NO `does not contain` / `fatal: error while running fast-import` in its transcript, then `git push` converges. If the description's claim ('the pull --rebase && push recovery reconciles after drift') were false - i.e. emit_import_stream still minted a parentless non-descendant commit - the assertion would fail because the transcript would capture the `fatal: error while running fast-import` string on the pull --rebase step and the ref would stay put (push a no-op, no convergence). The NEGATIVE GUARD assert further requires the pre-fix parentless emission to reproduce that exact fatal string as the captured RED baseline, so a future regression re-RED-s concretely rather than silently passing.
